content.full_text:
δ

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 19, 1989


Lester J. Marston
Rapport & Marston
200 Henry Street
P.O. Box 488
Ukiah, CA  95482

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-223

Dear Mr. Marston:

This letter confirms the telephone advice which was given to
you on May 5, 1989. You requested advice on behalf of Mr. Herbert
Giese concerning application of the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ to his duties as a
member of the Willis City Council. The following advice is based
upon the facts provided in your letters of April 11, 1989 and
April 20, 1989.

QUESTION

Under the Act, must Mr. Giese disqualify himself from
participating in a decision regarding a use permit for the Harwood
Energy Corporation, since both he and the Harwoods employ
Ms. Rowland?

CONCLUSION

Mr. Giese may participate in a decision regarding a use
permit for the Harwood Energy Corporation. Based on the facts
provided, neither Mr. Giese nor Giese Realty has a financial
interest in Ms. Rowland.

FACTS

Mr. Herbert Giese is a member of the Willis City Council,
and will be asked to vote on a use permit authorizing Harwood

_______________
1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

File No. A-89-223
Page 2

Energy Corporation to operate a cogeneration plant within the City
of Willis.

Mr. Giese is the owner of Giese Realty. He has two contracts
with Ms. Rita Rowland, a real estate agent. The first contract is
a broker-agent independent contractor employment contract. Under
this contract, Mr. Giese, a broker, employs Ms. Rowland, an agent,
to list and sell real estate. If Ms. Rowland sells any property
listed with Giese Realty, the commission is paid directly to
Mr. Giese, who then pays Ms. Rowland a percentage of the commission.

The second contract is a managing broker agreement. As of
January 1, 1988, Ms. Rowland assumed the responsibility of manager
of Giese Realty, managing all activities relating to real estate
and real estate sales persons. As compensation for these duties,
Ms. Rowland was paid 10 percent of the gross commissions received
by Giese Realty.

For the last 12 months, Ms. Rowland has also been employed by
Harwood Energy Corporation, which is owned by Morris and Bud
Harwood. She is a real estate consultant and is paid a salary of
$1,000 per month. She handles a variety of real estate matters,
which include evaluating and communicating purchase offers to the
Harwoods, reviewing and obtaining copies of documents, keeping
current records on all the properties and updating the property
taxes. In the last 12 months, Ms. Rowland has not acted as the
real estate agent for any sales on behalf of the Harwoods and
therefore has not earned any commission income.

The work that Ms. Rowland performs for the Harwoods is done
primarily at their office, which is about five blocks from Giese
Realty. However, on occasion she reviews documents and makes
phone calls on behalf of the Harwoods from the Giese Realty office. All such calls are billed to the Harwoods.

The Harwoods' properties are not listed with Giese Realty,
and none of Giese's real estate agents have sold any of the
Harwoods' properties within the last 12 months. None of the
income that Ms. Rowland has received under her written employment
contract with the Harwoods has been paid to Mr. Giese.

ANALYSIS

The Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in, or using his official position to influence a governmental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest. (Section 87100.) A public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the
official or a member of his immediate family or on, among other
things:

File No. A-89-223
Page 3

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dol-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, received
by or promised to the public official within 12
months prior to the time when the decision is made.

(d) Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

(Section 87103.)

Mr. Giese may not participate in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect on any business entity in which he has an interest or on
any person or entity which was a source of income to him within
the 12 months preceding the decision. (Section 87103(a), (c) and
(d).)

The question implicit in your letters concerns Ms. Rowland's
dual employment relationship with both Mr. Giese and the Harwoods,
and if this creates a conflict of interest which would prohibit
Mr. Giese from voting on the Harwood Energy Corporation use
permit. According to your April 20, 1989 letter, Ms. Rowland was
not the real estate agent for any sales on behalf of the Harwoods,
and none of the income that Ms. Rowland received from the Harwoods
was paid to Mr. Giese. Therefore, the Harwoods are not an indirect
source of income to Mr. Giese under Section 87103(c).

Furthermore, the Harwoods are also not a source of income to
Mr. Giese since none of the Harwoods' properties are listed with
Giese Realty nor have any of the Giese real estate agents sold
any of the Harwoods' properties within the past 12 months.

We must then determine if Ms. Rowland is a source of income
to Mr. Giese under the broker-agent independent contractor employment contract. Regulation 18704.3 defines sources of commission
income for both real estate brokers and real estate agents.
Regulation 18704.3(c) provides as follows:

File No. A-89-223
Page 4


     (c) The sources of commission income in a
specific sale or similar transaction include for
each of the following:

                           * * *

     (2) A real estate broker:

          (A) The person the broker
represents in the transaction;

          (B) If the broker receives a com-
mission from a transaction conducted by
an agent working under the broker's
auspices, the person represented by the
agent;

          (C) Any brokerage business entity
through which the broker conducts busi-
ness; and

          (D) Any person who receives a
finder's or other referral fee for refer-
ring a party to the transaction to the
broker, or who makes a referral pursuant
to a contract with the broker.

     (3) A real estate agent:

          (A) The broker and brokerage busi-
ness entity under whose auspices the
agent works;

          (B) The person the agent represents
in the transaction; and

          (C) Any person who receives a
finder's or other referral fee for refer-
ring a party to the transaction to the
broker, or who makes a referral pursuant
to a contract with the broker.

     According to the regulation, Mr. Giese is a source of income to
Ms. Rowland, but she is not a source of income to him.

     Therefore, Ms. Rowland's dual employment relationship with
both Mr. Giese and the Harwoods does not present Mr. Giese with a
financial interest which would affect his participating in or mak-
ing any decisions regarding the Harwood Energy Corporation use
permit.

File No. A-89-223
Page 5


If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Jill R. Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:JRS:plh

LAW OFFICES OF

RAPPORT & MARSTON

AN ASSOCIATION
OF
SOLE PRACTITIONERS

DAVID J. RAPPORT
LESTER J. MARSTON

200 Henry Street
P.O. Box 488
Ukiah, Ca. 95482
(707) 462-6846

April 11, 1989

Diane M. Griffiths, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

RE: Request for Advice Letter
Our File No. W-2.7

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

I am writing to you on behalf of my client, Herbert Giese,
who is a member of the Willits City Council. On May 8, 1989, Mr.
Giese will be asked to vote on a use permit authorizing Harwood
Energy Corporation to operate a cogeneration plant within the
City of Willits. Harwood Energy Corporation is owned by Morris
and Bud Harwood.

Mr. Giese is the owner of Giese Realty. In that capacity he
contracts with independent real estate brokers. One of those
brokers is Rita Rowland.

Under Ms. Rowland's contract with Mr. Giese, she pays him
10% of the gross profit that she receives from real estate sales
made through Mr. Giese's office. Under her contract she is entitled to utilize Mr. Giese's office and offer property for sale
under the listing of Giese Realty.

Within the last 12 months Ms. Rowland has been employed by
the Harwoods to assist them in a variety of real estate matters.
These real estate matters have included anything from obtaining
copies of documents such as deeds of trusts or foreclosure
notices to things as simple as taking documents to the Harwoods
for their signature. None of the work that Ms. Rowland has performed on the Harwood's behalf has included real estate sales.
Furthermore, Ms. Rowland has never paid Mr. Giese any money or
compensation for any of the work that she has performed on behalf
of the Harwoods.

I am writing to you now on behalf of Mr. Giese to request
that you render an opinion to Mr. Giese as to whether or not he
has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act which
prohibits him from voting on the Harwood use permit. Since the
vote on this item will take place on or about May 8th or 9th, I

Diane M. Griffiths, April 11, 1989, Page 2
------------------------------------------------------

would appreciate it if you could render an opinion on or before
that date.

If you need any additional assistance in order to act on
this request, please do not hesitate to give me a call.

Yours very truly,

LESTER J. MARSTON
City Attorney

RE: Request for Advice Letter
    Our File No. W-2.7

Dear Ms. Griffiths:

     I am writing to you on behalf of my client, Herbert Giese,
who is a member of the Willis City Council. On May 8, 1989, Mr.
Geise will be asked to vote on a use permit authorizing Harwood
Energy Corporation to operate a cogeneration plant within the
City of Willis.  Harwood Energy Corporation is owned by Norris
and Bud Harwood.

     Mr. Giese is the owner of Giese Realty. In that capacity he
contracts with independent real estate brokers. One of those
brokers is Rita Rowland.

     Under Ms. Rowland's contract with Mr. Giese, she pays him
10% of the gross profit that she receives from real estate sales
made through Mr. Giese's office. Under her contract she is entitled to utilize Mr. Giese's office and offer property for sale
under the listing of Giese Realty.

     Within the last 12 months Ms. Rowland has been employed by
the Harwoods to assist them in a variety of real estate matters.
These real estate matters have included anything from obtaining
copies of documents such as deeds of trusts or foreclosure
notices to things as simple as taking documents to the Harwoods
for their signature. None of the work that Ms. Rowland has performed on the Harwood's behalf has included real estate sales.
Furthermore, Ms. Rowland has never paid Mr. Giese any money or
compensation for any of the work that she has performed on behalf
of the Harwoods.

     I am writing to you now on behalf of Mr. Giese to request
that you render an opinion to Mr. Giese as to whether or not he
has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act which
prohibits him from voting on the Harwood use permit. Since the
vote on this item will take place on or about May 8th or 9th, I

Diane M. Griffiths, April 11, 1989, Page 2
-------------------------------------------------

would appreciate it if you could render an opinion on or before
that date.

If you need any additional assistance in order to act on
this request, please do not hesitate to give me a call.

Yours very truly,

[signature]

LESTER J. MARSTON
City Attorney

LJM/le
cc: Herb Giese

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 14, 1989


Lester Marston
Rapport & Marston
P.O. Box 488
Ukiah, CA 95482

Re: Letter No. 89-223

Dear Mr. Marston:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on April 13, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact Jill Stecher an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Jun 15 | 28 FM 98                FRIENDS OF COASTAL OPEN SPACE/
                                YES ON PROPOSITION E | 28 FM 98
                                P.O. BOX 91840
                                SANTA BARBARA, CA 93190


May 30, 1988

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

Dear FPPC representative,

This letter is in regard to contributions our committee has
received from Concerned Citizens to Save the Wilcox, an
unincorporated association. We have reported contributions of
$7,930 from this committee on our Form 420 for the period ended
May 21, 1988 (see pg 6 of 9).

Concerned Citizens to Save the Wilcox was awarded $30,000 in
settlement of a lawsuit with Santa Barbara Senior Housing
Associates Inc. The settlement specified that the $30,000 was to
be used on a campaign for Proposition C. As of May 29, our
committee has received $7,930 from the fiduciary holding the
settlement funds.

Our concerns are twofold. Firstly, we want to fully disclose the
nature of the contribution on our campaign reports. If our
method of disclosure is not complete, please let us know how to
amend our report.

Secondly, we are concerned about our potential status as a
sponsored committee as a result of receiving the contributions
from Concerned Citizens to Save the Wilcox. At the present time
those contributions represent 40% of our total cash receipts. If
we receive more contributions the percentage could go up to 70%
or more. Will you please respond to this concern?

Thank you for your help in these matters.


Sincerely,

Kathleen Modugno
Kathleen Modugno
Treasurer

California

June 17, 1988


Kathleen Modugno
Friends of Coastal Open Space/

P.O. Box 91840
Santa Barbara, CA  93190

Re:  88-223

Dear Ms. Modugno:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on June 15, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a

A public official has a 
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable 
that the decision will have a material financial effect, 
distinguishable from its effect on the public generally, on the 
official or a member of his immediate family or on, among other 
things:  
(a) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect investment worth 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more. 
(b) 
Any real property in which the public 
official has a direct or indirect interest worth 
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more. 
(c) 
Any source of income, other than gifts 
and other than loans by a commercial lending 
institution in the regular course of business on 
terms available to the public without regard to 
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty dol-
lars ($250) or more in value provided to, received 
by or promised to the public official within 12 
months prior to the time when the decision is made. 
(d) 
Any business entity in which the public 
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee, 
employee, or holds any position of management. 
(Section 87103.)

effect on any business entity in which he has an interest or on 
any person or entity which was a source of income to him within 
the 12 months preceding the decision.

Therefore, the Harwoods are not a indirect
